skill.name: minimax-pdf
skill.description: Create polished PDFs, fill PDF forms, and reformat existing documents using a local token-based design pipeline.
skill.summary: Generate, fill, and reformat print-ready PDFs with consistent visual design.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, and heuristic hits. Review confirms the skill intentionally runs a local PDF generation pipeline and can install dependencies, render HTML with Playwright, read input documents, and write output files. No prompt-injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found, but publication should require clear warnings about local code execution and network access.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution and Dependency Installation
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill intentionally runs shell, Python, Node, npm, npx, and Playwright commands. This is required for PDF generation, but it can modify the local environment and should only run in a trusted workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts directly execute package managers and Playwright setup commands. The context is legitimate document rendering, but the execution and installation behavior is confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Unescaped HTML Rendered in Headless Browser
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: Cover HTML interpolates title, subtitle, abstract, image URL, and design tokens without HTML escaping, then opens the generated file in Chromium. Malicious document metadata could run browser-side script or trigger external requests during rendering.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct interpolation into HTML is visible, and Playwright renders that HTML. Impact depends on who controls metadata and whether network access is available.

medium_findings.title: External Network Requests During Rendering and Setup
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill uses Google Fonts URLs in generated cover CSS and can install Playwright Chromium through npm or npx. Network access is expected, but it leaks render metadata timing and depends on external services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded Google Fonts URLs and dependency install commands are confirmed. They appear functional rather than malicious, but they are real network behaviors.

medium_findings.title: Broad Local File Read and Write Capabilities
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill reads user-provided documents, images, tokens, and JSON files, then writes PDFs and intermediate outputs to caller-provided paths. This is normal for a PDF tool but needs workspace and path controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are explicit and tied to user-supplied paths. No destructive intent is visible, but the capability is real.

low_findings.title: Credential and Malware Heuristic Hits Appear to Be False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static SAM, C2, weak crypto, and reconnaissance hits map to document design vocabulary, color names, generated labels, dependency checks, or examples. No evidence of credential theft, C2 infrastructure, or malicious crypto was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed locations show benign words such as SYSTEM_REPORT, Darkroom, font names, and design tokens. Confidence is not perfect because every listed duplicate was not individually re-read.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found in Reviewed Skill Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Searches of the main instructions, README, design notes, and scripts found no override language, fake system messages, claims of pre-approval, or requests to skip security analysis.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search did not find prompt-injection phrases, and reviewed instructions are ordinary usage guidance. This finding records absence of evidence, not a guarantee.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Manager Invocation from Skill Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can run pip, npm, and npx from local scripts. This pattern should be disclosed because it changes the execution environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Package installation commands are explicit in both shell and Python helper scripts.

dangerous_patterns.title: Headless Browser Rendering of Generated HTML
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill renders locally generated HTML with Playwright and Chromium. Combined with unescaped metadata and remote resources, this increases risk for untrusted input.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The browser rendering path is explicit and central to cover PDF creation.

content.value_statement: Basic PDF exports often look plain and inconsistent. This skill creates print-ready PDFs, fills forms, and reformats documents with a structured design pipeline.
actual_capabilities: Generates new PDFs from structured content blocks such as headings, tables, charts, images, code, math, and flowcharts.
Creates cover pages using token-based palettes, typography choices, and layout patterns.
Fills existing PDF form fields after inspecting available field names and allowed values.
Reformats Markdown, text, PDF, or JSON input into a styled PDF workflow.
Merges cover and body PDFs and reports page count and size checks.
Supports local customization for title, author, date, accent color, background color, abstract, and cover image.
limitations: Requires Python, Node.js, Playwright, Chromium, ReportLab, pypdf, and optional matplotlib for full output.
May install dependencies automatically, which is not suitable for locked-down environments.
Uses Google Fonts and optional cover image URLs, so rendering can make network requests.
PDF text extraction is best-effort and may not preserve complex source layouts.

use_cases.title: Client Proposal PDF
use_cases.description: Turn structured proposal content into a polished PDF with a designed cover, tables, and callouts.

use_cases.title: Fill Intake Forms
use_cases.description: Inspect fillable PDF fields, prepare values, and produce completed forms without changing the original layout.

use_cases.title: Reformat Existing Reports
use_cases.description: Convert Markdown, text, JSON, or extracted PDF text into a consistent report with professional typography.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic PDF
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a polished PDF titled "Quarterly Update" with a short executive summary, three bullets, and a simple table.

prompt_templates.title: Fill a PDF Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect this PDF form, list the fields, then fill the fields with the values I provide and save a completed copy.

prompt_templates.title: Reformat a Markdown Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Reformat this Markdown report into a print-ready PDF using a report style, a restrained accent color, charts, and numbered figures.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Designed Proposal
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a client-ready proposal PDF with a custom accent color, cover abstract, section callouts, comparison table, chart, bibliography, and final QA summary.

output_examples.input: A request to generate a strategy proposal with title, author, date, table, and chart content.
output_examples.output: A multi-page PDF with a designed cover, consistent headings, accent rules, formatted table, chart figure, and merged final output.

output_examples.input: A fillable form and values for name, agreement checkbox, country dropdown, and radio choice.
output_examples.output: A completed PDF form plus a concise summary of filled fields, skipped fields, and validation errors.

output_examples.input: A Markdown document with headings, bullets, code blocks, and references.
output_examples.output: A reformatted PDF with structured sections, readable body typography, preserved code formatting, and numbered references.

best_practices: Run dependency checks before generating a PDF, especially in a new environment.
Review extracted or converted content before final rendering when the source is a PDF.
Use trusted local image paths and approved external URLs for cover images and document figures.
anti_patterns: Do not run the automatic dependency installer in production or restricted workspaces without review.
Do not render untrusted metadata, abstracts, or image URLs without sanitizing them first.
Do not expect exact layout preservation when reformatting complex source PDFs.

faq.question: Can this skill create a PDF from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes. It builds a cover, body pages, and final merged PDF from structured content and document metadata.

faq.question: Can it fill existing PDF forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect fillable fields, then write values into text, checkbox, dropdown, listbox, and radio fields.

faq.question: Does it need network access?
faq.answer: Sometimes. Cover fonts use Google Fonts, cover images may use URLs, and dependency setup can use package managers.

faq.question: Does it execute local commands?
faq.answer: Yes. It runs shell, Python, Node.js, Playwright, and package manager commands as part of the PDF pipeline.

faq.question: Can it reformat an existing PDF perfectly?
faq.answer: No. Existing PDF parsing is best-effort text extraction, so complex layout should be reviewed before final rendering.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
